entity_types.name: MediaOutlet
entity_types.description: Tech or mainstream media organization that covers Apple, iPhone, and consumer tech; publishes news and reviews.

attributes.name: scope
attributes.description: Primary focus e.g. Apple-only, general tech

entity_types.name: Company
entity_types.description: For-profit business such as Apple, carriers, or device makers that can issue statements and run official accounts.

entity_types.name: OnlineCommunity
entity_types.description: Named online community or forum that aggregates and voices member sentiment e.g. subreddits or Tech Twitter.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Community handle or name e.g. r/iPhone

entity_types.name: Journalist
entity_types.description: Individual who reports or edits for tech media; drives news and opinion on Apple and iPhone.

entity_types.name: Analyst
entity_types.description: Industry or supply-chain analyst who publishes forecasts and leaks affecting rumor cycles.

entity_types.name: ContentCreator
entity_types.description: Creator who influences opinion via YouTube or social; reviews, unboxings, and commentary on iPhone.

entity_types.name: Developer
entity_types.description: Software or app developer whose work depends on iOS and iPhone hardware/API cycles.

entity_types.name: Advocate
entity_types.description: Individual who publicly advocates for causes such as right-to-repair, privacy, or accessibility.

entity_types.name: Person
entity_types.description: Any natural person not better classified as Journalist, Analyst, ContentCreator, Developer, or Advocate.

attributes.name: full_name
attributes.description: Display or real name when known

attributes.name: description
attributes.description: Brief role or context e.g. consumer, parent

entity_types.name: Organization
entity_types.description: Any institution or group not better classified as MediaOutlet, Company, or OnlineCommunity.

edge_types.name: WORKS_FOR
edge_types.description: Employment or contractual relationship between a person and an organization.

edge_types.name: REPORTS_ON
edge_types.description: Covers or comments on another entity or topic in a journalistic or analytical capacity.

edge_types.name: COMMENTS_ON
edge_types.description: Publicly comments, reviews, or gives opinion on another entity or topic.

edge_types.name: RESPONDS_TO
edge_types.description: Official or public response to another entity's statement or action.

edge_types.name: SUPPORTS
edge_types.description: Expresses support or alignment with another entity or position.

edge_types.name: OPPOSES
edge_types.description: Expresses opposition or criticism toward another entity or position.

edge_types.name: AFFILIATED_WITH
edge_types.description: Membership, affiliation, or representation of a community or organization.

edge_types.name: REGULATES
edge_types.description: Regulatory or oversight relationship between a body and an entity.

analysis_summary: The document describes a social-media simulation scenario around Apple skipping the iPhone 18 release in 2026. It emphasizes real speaking actors: tech media (outlets and journalists), analysts and leakers, content creators, developers, advocates, online communities (subreddits, Tech Twitter), and companies (Apple, carriers). The ontology uses 8 specific entity types (MediaOutlet, Company, OnlineCommunity, Journalist, Analyst, ContentCreator, Developer, Advocate) plus Person and Organization as fallbacks, and 8 relation types that capture employment, reporting, commenting, responding, support, opposition, affiliation, and regulation for Twitter/Reddit-style discourse.
